author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
How the Bishops Cleared Pelagius of Those Charges.
title: How the Bishops Cleared Pelagius of Those Charges.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: These four dogmas, thus connected with the name of Cœlestius, were therefore not approved by the bishops in their judgment, in the sense in which Cœlestius was said to have set them forth but in the sense which Pelagius gave to them in his reply.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: For they saw clearly enough, that it is one thing to be without sin, and another thing to live holily and righteously, as Scripture testifies that some lived even before the coming of Christ.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: And that although the Church here on earth is not without spot or wrinkle, she is yet both cleansed from every spot and wrinkle by the laver of regeneration, and in this state the Lord would have her continue.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: And continue she certainly will, for without doubt she shall reign without spot or wrinkle in an everlasting felicity.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: And that the perpetual virginity, which is not commanded, is unquestionably more than the purity of wedded life, which is commanded—although virginity is persevered in by many persons, who, notwithstanding, are not without sin.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: And that all those graces which he enumerates in a certain passage were possessed by the Apostle Paul; and yet, for all that, either they could quite understand, in regard to his having been worthy to receive them, that the merit was not according to his works, but rather, in some way, according to predestination (for the apostle says himself: “I am not meet to be called an apostle;”) or else their attention was not arrested by the sense which Pelagius gave to the word, as he himself viewed it.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Such are the points on which the bishops pronounced the agreement of Pelagius with the doctrines of godly truth.
